h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: So the card names the thing acting on the cart first, not the motion. Hand on cart, Earth on cart, surface on cart. The table card is the unclear one if the surface is already here.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: So the wording is still: one card names one interaction on the cart.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was on track to simply confirm the three-label list and wait for arrowhead instructions, continuing the forward procedural sequence without revisiting earlier reasoning.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Both cards reach the same contact. If this surface is the table, leave one contact label on the cart. Choose the card that stays, then read the cart's labels again.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: In response to L1's identification of the table/surface ambiguity as 'the unclear one,' the tutor abandons the original sequential card-placement task and introduces a new constraint — one contact, one label — physically enacting it by moving the table card outside the diagram, shifting from labelling procedure to a contact-uniqueness test.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Use that as the test now: each card must name one interaction on the cart, and a repeated contact leaves the diagram. Read the three labels again; no arrowheads yet.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: T3 ratifies the learner's revised framing by codifying 'one card names one interaction' as an explicit test rule, but it largely continues the prior lesson plan rather than inventing a new task or representation in direct response to L3's retrospective re-reading.
